# **Sonic Unleashed Recompiled \- Manual Archipelago World Logic**

## An APworld designed for the [Unleashed Recompiled](https://github.com/hedge-dev/UnleashedRecomp) project

![SonicUnleashedManualAPLogo](Images/image1.png)

# **Description**

This document was made to write down all the important information and logic regarding an apworld dedicated to the Sonic Unleashed ecosystem. It’ll go into expansive detail about how to translate a lot of the game’s elements into an Archipelago system, like the levels, medals, hot dog missions, etc. One thing to note is that this APworld was designed to originally work as a [**Manual Archipelago**](https://github.com/ManualForArchipelago/Manual), and so, some things in the world’s logic might favour that type of gameplay.  

If you’re reading this and are genuinely interested in expanding upon this by suggesting changes/features for the game world’s logic, I’d love to hear your thoughts\! And if by any chance you’d like to also take on the task to use this APworld as a base/inspiration or “as is” for your own Sonic Unleashed Recomp Archipelago integration, feel free to do so\! I’d be honored to help out and make this game have a functioning mod for standard Archipelago gameplay :D

# 

# **Table of Contents**

[**Setup**](#setup)

[**Options**](#options)

[**Rules & Important Notes**](#rules-&-important-notes)

[**Items**](#items)

[**Locations**](#locations)

# **Setup**

## **Step 0:** Requirements

To make this Manual Archipelago work, we’re going to need a couple of things first:

* A working build of [Sonic Unleashed Recomp](https://github.com/hedge-dev/UnleashedRecomp)  
* An [Archipelago Launcher](https://github.com/ArchipelagoMW/Archipelago/releases) (version preference of 0.6.6 or higher)  
* The Manual Sonic Unleashed .apworld file  
* A generated .YAML file for the Sonic Unleashed APWorld (look at the [**Generating a .YAML**](#step-3:-generating-a-.yaml) step to see how to make this file)  
* A [0% Save File with Story Completed](https://gamebanana.com/mods/585925) (made possible by [Gustavo Graziano](https://gamebanana.com/members/4316615), so huge thanks to them\!)  
  * Make sure to download the [**0\_save\_file\_no\_power\_ups\_and\_no\_upgrades.rar**](https://gamebanana.com/mods/download/585925#FileInfo_1427215) version, as this is the one we want to make unlocking certain items Archipelago compatible, such as the Upgrade Shoes.  
* **Optional:** The [Universal Tracker](https://github.com/FarisTheAncient/Archipelago/releases)

## 

## **Step 1:** Save File

First, we will replace our normal game’s save file with [Gustavo Graziano](https://gamebanana.com/members/4316615)’s [0% No Power Ups and No Upgrades](https://gamebanana.com/mods/download/585925#FileInfo_1427215) save file. To locate Unleashed Recomp’s save file folder, locate your computer’s AppData/Roaming folder (easily accessible by typing **Windows Key \+ R** and typing **%appdata%**)

![Screenshot1](Images/image8.png)

After doing this, you should find a folder called **UnleashedRecomp**. This is where your game’s save file is located. **If you wanna keep any old save file you have,** **now would be the time to do a backup of the content’s inside this folder to somewhere else**.

![Screenshot2](Images/image4.png)
	  
Now, find the contents inside the [**0\_save\_file\_no\_power\_ups\_and\_no\_upgrades.rar**](https://gamebanana.com/mods/download/585925#FileInfo_1427215) WinRar file, and paste them into the **UnleashedRecomp** folder, and consider this step done\!

![Screenshot3](Images/image6.png)

## **Step 2:** Installing the custom world

Now, assuming you’ve already downloaded the .apworld file for our Manual Archipelago, it’s just a matter of installing it in our Archipelago directory. You can do this by simply opening the file, or dragging the .apworld file into **Archipelago/custom\_worlds** folder.

![Screenshot4](Images/image5.png)

## **Step 3:** Generating a .YAML

Once the custom .apworld file is installed, we can actually use this to generate our .YAML file\! Simply open up your Archipelago Launcher, and find **Generate Template Options**.

![Screenshot5](Images/image7.png)

It’ll generate a .yaml file of all your installed games inside the **Archipelago/Players/Templates** folder, but the one we’re looking for is this one: **Manual\_SonicUnleashed\_Gabrimer.yaml**

![Screenshot6](Images/image2.png)

Now, place this .yaml file in the **Archipelago/Players** folder (the one folder before this one we’re in right now) and edit it to your heart's content\! Once that’s done, you’re ready to generate your multiworld session, assuming you have any other .yaml files you need for your intended playthrough.  
To read about all the options you can change in the .YAML file, head over to the [**Options**](#options) page. **(P.S: The way the Options are currently designed, some settings might make this .apworld impossible to generate if you wanted to play this Manual AP solo, without any other multiworlds considered into generation)**

## **Step 4 (Optional):** Utilizing the Universal Tracker

If you did end up getting the [Universal Tracker](https://github.com/FarisTheAncient/Archipelago/releases), great\! I personally think it does a **HUGE** favour to the **Manual’s Client**, which is high-lighting what is accessible for the player to go and clear out location checks, by marking them in green, and also displaying how many locations they can clear in each category. Have a look\!

![Screenshot7](Images/image3.png)

So how do you make this handy tool work? It’s really simple\! Just download the **tracker.apworld** file and install it into your Archipelago directory, just like we did to our Sonic Unleashed one. And that’s it\! When you run your Manual Client, things will be way more clear when playing. (specially if you have a lot of the [**Sanity Toggles**](#sanity-toggles) turned on)

# **Options**

These are all the different settings the player can change in their respective .YAML file for the multiworld.

## Progression Mode

There are two main types of progression mode for this apworld: [**Continent Mode**](#continent-mode), and [**Act Mode**](#act-mode). Each one works differently, and they function as follows:

### Continent Mode

In this mode, the player gets access to different regions of the game all together. The player gets Location Items Access, meaning they can access anything that’s inside that location, including Hub Stages, Act Stages, Boss Acts, DLC Acts and Hot Dog Trials (for the last two, assuming player also has [**DLC Acts Enabled**](#dlc-acts-enabled) and [**Hot Dog Trials Enabled**](#hot-dog-trials-enabled)).  
The player starts with: 

* Apotos Access.

	*(This is the Mode that’s turned **ON** by default)*

### Act Mode

In this mode, the player gets access to specific Acts in the game, those being base Hub Stages, Act Stages, Boss Acts, DLC Acts and Hot Dog Trials (for the last two, assuming player also has [**DLC Acts Enabled**](#dlc-acts-enabled) and [**Hot Dog Trials Enabled**](#hot-dog-trials-enabled)).  
The player starts with:

* Apotos Town Stage  
* Apotos Entrance Stage  
* Windmill Isle \- Day Act 1  
* Windmill Isle \- Day Act 2  
* Windmill Isle \- Night Act 1

It’s important to note that in **BOTH** modes, if the player were to include [**Medalsanity**](#medalsanity) into the world’s generation, they would also have to keep track of how many medals they have in the multiworld (not in the actual game itself) and compare with the act’s medal level to see if they can access it.

## DLC Acts Enabled

If turned **ON**, DLC Acts will be activated for the multiworld. If in [**Continent Mode**](#continent-mode), the player will only need a location’s access item to be able to play any DLC Act that is available in the game. If in [**Act Mode**](#act-mode), the player will need specific DLC Act Access items to play any of the DLC Acts.  
*(By default, this option is set to **OFF**.)*

## Hot Dog Trials Enabled

If turned **ON**, Hot Dog Trials will be activated for the multiworld. If in [**Continent Mode**](#continent-mode), the player will only need a location’s access item to be able to play any Hot Dog Trial that is available in the game. If in [**Act Mode**](#act-mode), the player will need specific Hot Dog Trials progressive items to be able to play any of the Hot Dog Trials.  
It should be noted that a player **CAN** bypass not having a hub’s access item if they do have a [**Hot Dog Trial**](#hot-dog-trials-\(progression\)) for a certain location, as those can’t be selected in the stage select unlike normal Acts, DLC Acts and Boss Acts. *(In a perfect world, I could see each Hot Dog Trial and it’s respective levels being selectable in a location’s level select in a normal Modded Archipelago).*  
*(By default, this option is set to **OFF**.)*

## Sanity Toggles

There’s a couple of Sanity Toggles for Sonic Unleashed, these being options the player can choose to include in their multiworld or not. The term “Sanity” comes from multiple AP worlds having a certain amount of items/locations that are really intense to go through.

### Medalsanity

This toggle changes up significantly how the game progression works. Players now have to look out for medals to collect (since there are so many, there’s no doubt some important items from the Item Pool will be sent to these medal locations), and players will also have to respect the medals they have received inside the multiworld to be able to access certain acts in ANY type of [**Progression Mode**](#progression-mode).  
*(By default, this option is set to **OFF**.)*

### Souvenirsanity

This toggle adds a small change to game progression, where the player can receive souvenirs that get thrown into the multiworld’s Item Pool. Whenever a player receives a souvenir, they made trade it with Professor Pickle to send out a location check.  
*(By default, this option is set to **OFF**.)*

### Collectiblesanity

This toggle adds a small change to game progression, where the player can receive collectibles that get thrown into the multiworld’s Item Pool. The collectibles themselves are considered filler, but the in-game collectibles the player can find can send out location checks.  
*(By default, this option is set to **OFF**.)*

### S Rank-sanity

This toggle makes it so every act present in the game gets an S Rank location variant that only gets checked if the player achieves the S Rank in that act.  
*(By default, this option is set to **OFF**.)*

# **Rules & Important Notes**

So, there’s a couple of things to keep in mind while playing this Sonic Unleashed Archipelago, as mentioned before, this is a **Manual**. This section just goes over a few details to remember as you’re playing the game.  
If any questions come to mind either from these existing rules, or something I did not go over myself, please make sure to ask me over at Discord, either in DMs or the Manual AP Sonic Unleashed post\!

## The Goal

Within every Archipelago game, there’s always an end goal location, and Sonic Unleashed is no different to that\! The main goal is to defeat [**Dark Gaia**](#final-boss-clear), which the player will need all [**7 Chaos Emeralds**](#chaos-emerald-\(progression\)) to do. They receive these items once they defeat each of the respective [**Gaia Temple Bosses**](#boss-act-clear) of a said region. Once they have all of those (and access to Eggmanland too, of course) they are allowed to go beat their game\!

## LV Upgrades

As you may notice, the [0% No Power Ups and No Upgrades](https://gamebanana.com/mods/download/585925#FileInfo_1427215) save file contains 99 LVs of upgrades right off the bat. This is actually a good thing for us\! As the game includes [**LV Upgrade**](#lv-upgrade-\(useful\)) items that you can receive, the point is to only level up Sonic if you receive an item for it, whether for regular [**Sonic**](#sonic-the-hedgehog), or [**Werehog**](#sonic-the-werehog) form.  
There’s no way to keep a run from having infinite XP throughout a session, but with the way and frequency you receive these items, I find it hard to believe a player will run out of XP, thus not being able to upgrade if they get more LV Upgrade items. (If this is the case, a good way to counter this is buying a lot of food items for XP, as the save file comes with a bunch of rings).

## Shoe Upgrades

Another cool thing the [0% No Power Ups and No Upgrades](https://gamebanana.com/mods/download/585925#FileInfo_1427215) save file features, is not having all of Sonic’s [**Shoe Upgrade**](#shoe-upgrade-items-\(progression\)) items from the get-go. Meaning, we can include them in item pool generation\! The player should only go get a certain shoe if they get it in the multiworld.  
**I have noticed some shoes seem rather difficult or downright impossible to get if the player doesn’t have a previous shoe upgrade, such as the [Air Boost Shoes](#air-boost-shoes) from Chun-nan requiring the [Wall Jump Shoes](#wall-jump-shoes) to reach it, and that one requiring the [Stomping Shoes](#stomping-shoes) as well.**  
**A direct quote from the [Air Boost Shoes](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Air_Boost_Shoes) wiki page says:** *[“In order to obtain it, Sonic must possess the Wall Jump Shoes Level Up Item.“*](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Air_Boost_Shoes#:~:text=In%20order%20to%20obtain%20it%2C%20Sonic%20must%20possess%20the%20Wall%20Jump%20Shoes%20Level%20Up%20Item%2E)**, so further testing is required to know if this logic should maintain existing in the Manual Archipelago. If you have the time to spare, and would like to help out, please give it a try and let me know what you discover\!**

## Hub Stage Access Items

During [**Continent Mode**](#continent-mode), players can receive [**Location Access**](#location-access-\(progression\)) items, and in [**Act Mode**](#act-mode), players can receive [**Hub Stage Access**](#hub-stages-access-\(progression\)) items, both which serve as a way to let players into either [**Town Stages**](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Sonic_Unleashed_\(Xbox_360/PlayStation_3\)_Town_Stages), or [**Entrance Stages**](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Entrance_Stage). The reason this item was made, was to gate players from being able to enter any Hub Stage from the start.  
However, this can be an issue if the player also has [**Hot Dog Trials Enabled**](#hot-dog-trials-enabled), as you can only enter those missions via the Town Stages. The same can also be said when going to collect a [**Shoe Upgrade**](#shoe-upgrade-items-\(progression\)), since this is a **Manual Archipelago**, players must go pick up their Shoe Upgrade item in-game, and they could not possess the access to these Entrance Stages.  
**For the time being, players are to ignore this rule if these conditions are met. In a perfect world, I could see each level of a [Hot Dog Trial](#hot-dog-trials-\(progression\)) being able to be accessed via a location’s level select screen, and [Shoe Upgrades](#shoe-upgrade-items-\(progression\)) being automatically added to Sonic’s kit, if a regular Modded Archipelago Sonic Unleashed build were to be made.**

# **Items**

The items are all the things the player can get for Sonic Unleashed. Most items are things related to making the player be able to progress in the game. A lot of items that do get added into the Item Pool vary depending on the settings the player has enabled or disabled in their YAML settings.

## [Location Access](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Sonic_Unleashed_\(Xbox_360/PlayStation_3\)_Town_Stages) (Progression)

The Location Access category are all items related to letting the player enter a location’s hub, stage select screen and hot dog trials when playing [**Continent Mode**](#continent-mode). If the player were to include [**Medalsanity**](#medalsanity) into the world’s generation, they would also have to keep track of how many medals they have in the multiworld (not in the actual game itself) and compare with the act’s medal level to see if they can access it. (Medal count doesn’t apply to DLC levels).

* ### Adabat Access

* ### Apotos Access

* ### Chun-nan Access

* ### Eggmanland Access

* ### Empire City Access

* ### Holoska Access

* ### Mazuri Access

* ### Shamar Access

* ### Spagonia Access

## 

## [Hub Stages Access](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Sonic_Unleashed_\(Xbox_360/PlayStation_3\)_Town_Stages) (Progression) 

The Hub Stages Access category are all items related to letting the player enter a location’s hub when playing [**Act Mode**](#act-mode). These Stage items are either Town Stages, or Entrance Stages. It should be noted that a player **CAN** bypass not having a hub’s access item if they do have a [**Hot Dog Trial**](#hot-dog-trials-\(progression\)) for a certain location, as those can’t be selected in the stage select unlike normal Acts, DLC Acts and Boss Acts. *(In a perfect world, I could see each Hot Dog Trial and it’s respective levels being selectable in a location’s level select in a normal Modded Archipelago game).*  
The main reason a player would want to enter either a Town Stage or Entrance stage would be to collect its Medals, Souvenirs and/or Collectibles (although this is only relevant if they have the respective [**Sanity Toggle**](#sanity-toggles) on)

### Adabat

* Adabat Town Stage  
* Adabat Entrance Stage

### Apotos

* Apotos Town Stage  
* Apotos Entrance Stage

### Chun-nan

* Chun-nan Town Stage  
* Chun-nan Entrance Stage

### Eggmanland

* Eggmanland Town Stage

### Empire City

* Empire City Town Stage  
* Empire City Entrance Stage

### Holoska

* Holoska Town Stage  
* Holoska Entrance Stage

### Mazuri

* Mazuri Town Stage  
* Mazuri Entrance Stage

### Shamar

* Shamar Town Stage  
* Shamar Entrance Stage

### Spagonia

* Spagonia Town Stage  
* Spagonia Entrance Stage

## 

## [Act Access](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Sonic_Unleashed_\(Xbox_360/PlayStation_3\)_Action_Stages) (Progression)

The Act Access category are all the items related to letting the player enter a Day or Night stage (excluding DLC levels) when playing [**Act Mode**](#act-mode). Just like the [**Location Access**](#location-access-\(progression\)) category, if the player were to include [**Medalsanity**](#medalsanity) into the world’s generation, they would also have to keep track of how many medals they have in the multiworld (not in the actual game itself) and compare with the act’s medal level to see if they can access it.

### Jungle Joyride (Adabat Acts)

* Jungle Joyride \- Day Act 2  
* Jungle Joyride \- Day Act 3  
* Jungle Joyride \- Night Act 1  
* Jungle Joyride \- Night Act 2

* Windmill Isle \- Day Act 2

* Windmill Isle \- Night Act 1

## [Boss Act Access](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Gaia_Temple#Locations) (Progression)

The Boss Act Access category are all the items related to letting the player enter a Day or Night boss stage when playing [**Act Mode**](#act-mode). These are the guardians of the Gaia Temples, and once defeated, they will give the player a [**Chaos Emerald**](#chaos-emerald-\(progression\)) item.

* ### [Egg Lancer](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Egg_Lancer)

* ### [Dark Gaia Phoenix](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Dark_Gaia_Phoenix)

* ### [Egg Dragoon](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Egg_Dragoon#Sonic_Unleashed)

* ### [Dark Moray](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Dark_Moray)

* ### [Egg Beetle](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Egg_Beetle)

* ### [Dark Guardian](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Dark_Guardian)

* ### [Egg Devil Ray](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Egg_Devil_Ray)

## [Chaos Emerald](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Gaia_Temple#Locations) (Progression)

The Chaos Emerald category are all of the 7 Chaos Emerald items the player gets for beating each of the Gaia Temple guardians (that means these are the only set of items to NOT be randomized in the multiworld, and instead are always in their respective boss locations).  
All 7 Chaos Emeralds are required to defeat the Final Boss, [**Dark Gaia**](#final-boss-clear), which is the player’s main goal.

* ### Purple Chaos Emerald

* ### Red Chaos Emerald

* ### White Chaos Emerald

* ### Cyan Chaos Emerald

* ### Green Chaos Emerald

* ### Yellow Chaos Emerald

* ### Blue Chaos Emerald

## [DLC Act Access](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Sonic_Unleashed_\(Xbox_360/PlayStation_3\)_Action_Stages) (Progression)

The DLC Act Access category are all the items related to letting the player enter a Day or Night DLC stage if the player were to activate the [**DLC Acts Enabled**](#dlc-acts-enabled) option in their YAML file and were playing [**Act Mode**](#act-mode).

## 

## [Hot Dog Trials](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Mission_\(Sonic_Unleashed\)#Don_Fachio_Missions) (Progression)

The Hot Dog Trials items are all the items related to letting the player access the Hot Dog Trials for each location. Since the trials include up to 3 different levels of difficulty, and these cannot be accessed in the stage select (only LVL 3), the items are considered “progressive”.  
What that means is that, if a player gets a progressive hot dog trial for a certain location, they can do the first level of that specific place, and if they get a new progressive item for that trial, they can do the second level, and the same goes for the third.  
These items will be only generated in the multiworld if the player decides to activate the [**Hot Dog Trials Enabled**](#hot-dog-trials-enabled) option in their YAML file options.

### Jungle Joyride (Hot Dog Trials)

* Jungle Joyride \- Day Time Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Jungle Joyride \- Day Battle Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Jungle Joyride \- Day Ring Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Jungle Joyride \- Night Time Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Jungle Joyride \- Night Survival Progressive (x3)

### Windmill Isle (Hot Dog Trials)

* Windmill Isle \- Day Time Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Windmill Isle \- Day Battle Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Windmill Isle \- Day Ring Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Windmill Isle \- Night Time Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Windmill Isle \- Night Survival Progressive (x3)

### Dragon Road (Hot Dog Trials)

* Dragon Road \- Day Time Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Dragon Road \- Day Battle Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Dragon Road \- Day Ring Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Dragon Road \- Night Time Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Dragon Road \- Night Survival Progressive (x3)

### Eggmanland (Hot Dog Trials)

* Eggmanland \- Time Trial Progressive (x3)

### Skyscraper Scamper (Hot Dog Trials)

* Skyscraper Scamper \- Day Time Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Skyscraper Scamper \- Day Battle Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Skyscraper Scamper \- Day Ring Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Skyscraper Scamper \- Night Time Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Skyscraper Scamper \- Night Survival Progressive (x3)

### Cool Edge (Hot Dog Trials)

* Cool Edge \- Day Time Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Cool Edge \- Day Battle Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Cool Edge \- Day Ring Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Cool Edge \- Night Time Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Cool Edge \- Night Survival Progressive (x3)

### Savannah Citadel (Hot Dog Trials)

* Savannah Citadel \- Day Time Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Savannah Citadel \- Day Battle Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Savannah Citadel \- Day Ring Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Savannah Citadel \- Night Time Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Savannah Citadel \- Night Survival Progressive (x3)

### Arid Sands (Hot Dog Trials)

* Arid Sands \- Day Time Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Arid Sands \- Day Battle Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Arid Sands \- Day Ring Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Arid Sands \- Night Time Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Arid Sands \- Night Survival Progressive (x3)

* Rooftop Run \- Day Time Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Rooftop Run \- Day Battle Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Rooftop Run \- Day Ring Trial Progressive (x3)  
* Rooftop Run \- Night Time Trial Progressive (x3)

## 

## Missions Access ***(Currently Unused)***

The Missions Access items are all the items related to letting the player access the missions found in each location’s hub. Currently unused, but would allow the player to do a certain NPC’s mission if **Missionsanity** were to be turned on in the player’s YAML file settings.

## 

## [Shoe Upgrade Items](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Sonic_Unleashed_Level_Up_Items) (Progression)

Shoe Upgrade Items are highly important progression items, as without them, Sonic physically cannot finish certain acts in the game.  
***\***The way to deal with the manual archipelago’s logic with this, would be to collect the specific shoe upgrade once the player were to receive the item.*

* ### [Air Boost Shoes](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Air_Boost_Shoes)

* ### [Light Speed Shoes](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Light_Speed_Shoes)

* ### [Stomping Shoes](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Stomping_Shoes)

* ### [Wall Jump Shoes](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Wall_Jump_Shoes)

## 

## [Uncanny Camera](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Exorcisms) ***(Currently Unused)***

The Uncanny Camera is an item that allows the player to perform Exorcism missions on NPCs around the hubs. Currently unused, but would be considered a Progression item and only included in multiworld if the **Exorcismsanity** toggle was turned on.

## [Medal Items](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Medal#Sonic_Unleashed) (Progression)

The Medals are the main progression item for this game when [**Medalsanity**](#medalsanity) is turned on. As this is a Manual Archipelago, the way to get around the medal quantity is by honoring how many medals the player got in the multiworld, and not in the game itself (as in the game, you just get medals normally by playing the stages).  
If the player has collected enough medals in-game, but doesn’t have enough in the multiworld, the player should honor the multiworld, by not being able to play that certain act. On the other end of the spectrum, if the player doesn’t have enough medals in-game, but enough in the multiworld, they are free to select the level in the stage select inside the world hub. There are a total of **200** Sun Medals and **200** Moon Medals.

Here’s a quick sheet to help players know if they have enough medals for a stage:

| Medal Level | No. of Medals |
| :---: | :---: |
| 1 | 0-14 |
| 2 | 15-29 |
| 3 | 30-44 |
| 4 | 45-59 |
| 5 | 60-79 |
| 6 | 80-119 |
| 7 | 120-200 |

## 

## [Souvenir Items](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Souvenir) (Progression)

Although seemingly worthless at first, souvenirs can come in handy when gifting them to Professor Pickle, as he can give the player either sun or moon medals, which would mark location checks **(this only applies if [Medalsanity](#medalsanity) is turned on)**.  
Probably something really rare to happen in a run, and you’d rarely need to actually trade these items for medal location checks, but since the chance **still** *exists*, these items are to be considered progression.  
	***\***The way to deal with the manual archipelago’s logic with this, would be to buy the item in the shop once you receive it (almost like these items are to be considered “Souvenir Items Purchase Access")*

### Adabat Souvenirs

* Photo Frame (Adabat)  
* Miniature Flag (Adabat)  
* Hammock  
* Chehaya Charm  
* Anji Sculpture

### Apotos Souvenirs

* Photo Frame (Apotos)  
* Miniature Flag (Apotos)  
* Potpourri  
* Lace Tablecloth  
* Water Jug

### Chun-nan Souvenirs

* Photo Frame (Chun-Nan)  
* Miniature Flag (Chun-nan)  
* Censer  
* Lantern  
* Urn

### Eggmanland Souvenirs

* Photo Frame (Eggmanland)  
* Miniature Flag (Eggmanland)  
* Egg Balloon  
* Eggman Poster  
* Egg Goggles

### 

### Empire City Souvenirs

* Photo Frame (Empire City)  
* Miniature Flag (Empire City)  
* Magazine  
* Jenny  
* Jack-o'-Lantern

### Holoska Souvenirs

* Photo Frame (Holoska)  
* Miniature Flag (Holoska)  
* Nesting Dolls  
* Model Sled  
* Whale Ornament

### Mazuri Souvenirs

* Photo Frame (Mazuri)  
* Miniature Flag (Mazuri)  
* Earthenware Pot  
* Equine Carving  
* Talismanic Mask

### Shamar Souvenirs

* Photo Frame (Shamar)  
* Miniature Flag (Shamar)  
* Shamaran Sand  
* Lunisolar Charm  
* Carpet

### Spagonia Souvenirs

* Photo Frame (Spagonia)  
* Miniature Flag (Spagonia)  
* Maschera  
* Spagonian Glass  
* Spagonian Cameo

## 

## [LV Upgrade](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Experience_Point#Sonic_Unleashed) (Useful)

The LV Upgrade item is how Sonic will level up his character stats during this multiworld playthrough. As the player should be using the provided save file that includes up to 99 levels of XP to use, they should rarely run out of this during gameplay. Whenever the player receives a LV Upgrade item, they should level up Sonic on that specific parameter they got.

### Sonic The Hedgehog

* LV Upgrade Progressive \- Speed (x10)  
* LV Upgrade Progressive \- Ring Energy (x5)

### Sonic The Werehog

* LV Upgrade Progressive \- Combo (x30)  
* LV Upgrade Progressive \- Strength (x10)  
* LV Upgrade Progressive \- Life (x10)  
* LV Upgrade Progressive \- Unleash (x10)  
* LV Upgrade Progressive \- Shield (x10)

## 

## Collectible Items (Filler)

Collectibles are all the items that Sonic doesn’t need to progress in the game. These include Art Books, Records, Videotapes, Souvenirs, etc.

### Art Related Collectibles

* [Art Book](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Art_Book) (x71)  
* [Bookshelf](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Bookshelf)

### Song Related Collectibles

* [Record](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Record) (x62)  
* [Gramophone](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Gramophone)  
* [Audio System](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Audio_System)

### Video Related Collectibles

* [Videotape](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Videotape) (x59)  
* [TV](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/TV)

# **Locations**

The locations are all the things the player can do to send checks to the multiworld. These locations' accessibility vary a lot depending on the settings the player has enabled or disabled in their YAML file.

## [Act Clear](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Sonic_Unleashed_\(Xbox_360/PlayStation_3\)_Action_Stages)

The Act Clear locations are all the levels (excluding DLC) that the player can beat to send out checks in the multiworld. If in [**Continent Mode**](#continent-mode), players can access any Act from any location they have access to. If in [**Act Mode**](#act-mode), players can only enter Acts they have directed received access to. And in both modes, if [**Medalsanity**](#medalsanity) is turned on, players have to keep track of how many medals they have in the multiworld to see if they’re allowed to play an Act.

### Jungle Joyride (Adabat Acts)

* Jungle Joyride \- Day Act 1 \- CLEAR  
* Jungle Joyride \- Day Act 2 \- CLEAR  
* Jungle Joyride \- Day Act 3 \- CLEAR  
* Jungle Joyride \- Night Act 1 \- CLEAR  
* Jungle Joyride \- Night Act 2 \- CLEAR

### Windmill Isle (Apotos Acts)

* Windmill Isle \- Day Act 1 \- CLEAR  
* Windmill Isle \- Day Act 2 \- CLEAR  
* Windmill Isle \- Day Act 3 \- CLEAR  
* Windmill Isle \- Night Act 1 \- CLEAR  
* Windmill Isle \- Night Act 2 \- CLEAR

### Dragon Road (Chun-nan Acts)

* Dragon Road \- Day Act 1 \- CLEAR  
* Dragon Road \- Day Act 2 \- CLEAR  
* Dragon Road \- Day Act 3 \- CLEAR  
* Dragon Road \- Night Act 1 \- CLEAR  
* Dragon Road \- Night Act 2 \- CLEAR

### Eggmanland (Eggmanland Acts)

* Eggmanland \- CLEAR  
* Tornado Defense: Act 2 \- CLEAR

### Skyscraper Scamper (Empire City Acts)

* Skyscraper Scamper \- Day Act 1 \- CLEAR  
* Skyscraper Scamper \- Day Act 2 \- CLEAR  
* Skyscraper Scamper \- Night Act 1 \- CLEAR

### Cool Edge (Holoska Acts)

* Cool Edge \- Day Act 1 \- CLEAR  
* Cool Edge \- Day Act 2 \- CLEAR  
* Cool Edge \- Night Act 1 \- CLEAR

### Savannah Citadel (Mazuri Acts)

* Savannah Citadel \- Day Act 1 \- CLEAR  
* Savannah Citadel \- Day Act 2 \- CLEAR  
* Savannah Citadel \- Day Act 3 \- CLEAR  
* Savannah Citadel \- Night Act 1 \- CLEAR  
* Savannah Citadel \- Night Act 2 \- CLEAR

### Arid Sands (Shamar Acts)

* Arid Sands \- Day Act 1 \- CLEAR  
* Arid Sands \- Day Act 2 \- CLEAR  
* Arid Sands \- Night Act 1 \- CLEAR

### Rooftop Run (Spagonia Acts)

* Rooftop Run \- Day Act 1 \- CLEAR  
* Rooftop Run \- Day Act 2 \- CLEAR  
* Rooftop Run \- Day Act 3 \- CLEAR  
* Tornado Defense: Act 1 \- CLEAR  
* Rooftop Run \- Night Act 1 \- CLEAR

## [Boss Act Clear](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Gaia_Temple#Locations)

The Boss Act Clear locations are all the boss acts the player can beat to win a Chaos Emerald, advancing their progress towards their main goal. If in [**Continent Mode**](#continent-mode), players can access any Boss Act from any location they have access to. If in [**Act Mode**](#act-mode), players can only enter Boss Acts they have directed received access to.

* ### [Egg Lancer \- CLEAR](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Egg_Lancer)

* ### [Dark Gaia Phoenix \- CLEAR](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Dark_Gaia_Phoenix)

* ### [Egg Dragoon \- CLEAR](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Egg_Dragoon#Sonic_Unleashed)

* ### [Dark Moray \- CLEAR](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Dark_Moray)

* ### [Egg Beetle \- CLEAR](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Egg_Beetle)

* ### [Dark Guardian \- CLEAR](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Dark_Guardian)

* ### [Egg Devil Ray \- CLEAR](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Egg_Devil_Ray)

## [Final Boss Clear](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Dark_Gaia)

The Final Boss Clear is the victory goal for the player to reach in their game. To be able to access this location, players must have collected all 7 [**Chaos Emeralds**](#chaos-emerald-\(progression\)) by defeating all of the Gaia Temple guardians.

## 

## [DLC Act Clear](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Category:Sonic_Unleashed_\(Xbox_360/PlayStation_3\)_Action_Stages)

The DLC Act Clear locations are all the DLC levels that the player can beat to send out checks in the multiworld if [**DLC Acts Enabled**](#dlc-acts-enabled) is turned **ON**. If in [**Continent Mode**](#continent-mode), players can access any DLC Act from any location they have access to. If in [**Act Mode**](#act-mode), players can only enter DLC Acts they have directed received access to.

## [Hot Dog Trials Clear](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Mission_\(Sonic_Unleashed\)#Don_Fachio_Missions)

Hot Dog Trials Clear locations are all the time, battle ring and survival trials that the player can beat to send out checks in the multiworld. To access a specific trial you have access to, go to the trial’s specific location hub, talk to the chili dog vendor and purchase the trial you wish to play. These locations will be only present in the multiworld if the player decides to activate the [**Hot Dog Trials Enabled**](#hot-dog-trials-enabled) option in their YAML file options.

## 

## [Medal Locations](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Medal#Sonic_Unleashed)

The medal locations are all **200** sun and **200** moon medals the player can find throughout the game, sending checks to the multiworld when collecting them. Medals can be found in hubs, stages, certain missions and even by giving Professor Pickle souvenirs. These locations are only accessible if [**Medalsanity**](#medalsanity) is turned on in the player’s YAML file settings.

## 

## [Souvenir Locations](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Souvenir)

The souvenir locations are all **45** souvenirs the player can buy in each different location. Once a souvenir is traded with Professor Pickle, a check is sent to the multiworld. These locations only exist in the multiworld if **Souvenirsanity** is turned on in the player’s YAML file settings.

| Name | Shop |
| :---: | :---: |
| Photo Frame (Spagonia) | [Josef's](https://sonic.fandom.com/wiki/Josef%27s) |

## Collectible Locations

The collectible locations are all **71** art books, **62** records and **59** videotapes the player can find/collect in the game. Once a player snatches one of these, it sends a check in the multiworld. These locations only exist in the multiworld if **Collectiblesanity** is turned on in the player’s YAML file settings.

## 

## S Rank Locations

The S Rank locations are extra checks the player can enable in their YAML file settings by turning on [**S Rank-sanity**](#s-rank-sanity). It makes it so every act present in the game gets an S Rank location variant that only gets checked if the player achieves the S Rank in that act.  
If [**DLC Acts Enabled**](#dlc-acts-enabled) were to be turned **ON**, the multiworld will include S-Rank location checks for those as well.

### Jungle Joyride (Adabat Acts)

* Jungle Joyride \- Day Act 1 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Jungle Joyride \- Day Act 1-2 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Jungle Joyride \- Day Act 2 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Jungle Joyride \- Day Act 3 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Jungle Joyride \- Day Act 4 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Jungle Joyride \- Day Act 5 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Egg Lancer \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Jungle Joyride \- Night Act 1 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Jungle Joyride \- Night Act 1-2 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Jungle Joyride \- Night Act 2 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Jungle Joyride \- Night Act 3 \- S-Rank CLEAR

### Windmill Isle (Apotos Acts)

* Windmill Isle \- Day Act 1 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Windmill Isle \- Day Act 1-2 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Windmill Isle \- Day Act 2 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Windmill Isle \- Day Act 2-2 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Windmill Isle \- Day Act 3 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Windmill Isle \- Day Act 4 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Windmill Isle \- Night Act 1 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Windmill Isle \- Night Act 1-2 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Windmill Isle \- Night Act 1-3 \- S-Rank CLEAR  
* Windmill Isle \- Night Act 2 \- S-Rank CLEAR
